title: Button — Shared Interactive Button Component
problem: The app needs consistent interactive button styling across all screens with reliable touch targets (≥44px), accessible focus states, loading states, and semantic variants (primary, danger, ghost) that don't need to be reimplemented per screen.
analysis: Shared Button component with variant system. All buttons: minimum 44px touch target height, font-weight 500, system font, cursor-pointer. Variants: `primary` (amber bg, dark text), `danger` (red bg, white text), `ghost` (transparent bg, text-secondary), `icon` (square, no text). `disabled` and `loading` states built-in (opacity 60%, pointer-events-none when disabled; spinner replaces label when loading). ButtonLink is the same styles applied to a Next.js `<Link>` for navigation actions.
successCriteria:
- All buttons ≥ 44px tall
- Primary variant: amber bg (#f59e0b), dark text
- Danger variant: danger red bg, white text
- Ghost variant: transparent, text-secondary
- disabled prop: opacity-60, pointer-events-none, aria-disabled
- loading prop: spinner replaces label, button remains disabled
- Full-width option via `fullWidth` prop
- ButtonLink renders a Next.js Link with same styling
implementation: components/ui/Button.tsx: `variant`, `disabled`, `loading`, `fullWidth`, `children`, standard button HTML props. components/ui/ButtonLink.tsx: same props + `href`, renders Next.js `<Link>`. Spinner: small white/dark svg rotating animation.

decisions.decision: ButtonLink uses Next.js Link, not <a href>
decisions.rationale: Using <a href> for in-app navigation triggers full page reload. Next.js Link performs a client-side transition.

knownLimitations: Loading spinner color is fixed (white on primary/danger, text-secondary on ghost) — not customizable per call
userGuide: Buttons throughout the app use a consistent amber (primary), red (danger), or subtle (ghost) style. All buttons are large enough to tap comfortably on mobile.
